name: Southern Turnip Green Soup (Florida Cooking)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the turnip roots off the greens and discard any blemished or yellowed edges.
2. Tear the large leaves into smaller pieces and trim the ends of the turnips, peeling them to create bite-size pieces (about 4 cups total).
3. Melt 3 tablespoons butter in a large skillet over moderate heat. Add the cubed turnips and sauté until crisp-tender, about 10 minutes.
4. Add the chopped onions and sauté for another 5 minutes or until tender but not browned.
5. Stir in 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon sugar, and 1 1/2 cups chicken stock. Cook until well blended.
6. Add the lemon juice and let the mixture cool slightly.
7. In a separate skillet, melt the remaining 3 tablespoons butter over medium heat. Add the greens and sauté for about 10 minutes until crisp-tender.
8. Stir in 1 teaspoon salt and 1 teaspoon sugar, then add the remaining chicken stock. Cook until well blended and let cool for 15 minutes.
9. Puree the greens in batches using a food processor or blender.
10. Combine the pureed greens with the turnip mixture, mixing thoroughly.
11. Reheat the soup gently, then stir in the half-and-half and cooked grits. Adjust the salt and pepper to taste before serving hot.
